title: eryngium tricuspidatum subsp. subintegrum
intro: Eryngium tricuspidatum subsp. subintegrum is a subspecies of the Eryngium tricuspidatum plant, a type of flowering plant in the carrot family. This plant, also known as the three-lobed sea holly, is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with Eryngium tricuspidatum subsp. subintegrum include its ability to support respiratory health, reduce inflammation, and alleviate digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
